patches.hypothesis: The bug is likely due to the stop method setting the stopTime regardless of whether the stopwatch was suspended or running. This causes the total time to be incorrect when the stopwatch is stopped after being suspended.

The bug occurs because the stop method sets the stopTime regardless of whether the stopwatch was suspended or actively running. When the stopwatch is suspended, it should not update the stopTime upon stopping.
patches.changes:
- stopTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
+ if (this.runningState == STATE_RUNNING) stopTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
patches.diff:
- stopTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
+ if (this.runningState == STATE_RUNNING) stopTime = System.currentTimeMillis();

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the stop method resetting the stopTime when the stopwatch is stopped after being suspended. The fix is to ensure that the stopTime is not reset if the stopwatch was previously suspended.
